what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the lifters registry. It declares that a lifter is a file-discovered, self-registering deterministic extractor that produces a produced_by:"code" projection without a model call, lists the current lifters, and prescribes how to add, author, and discover them.
observations: Lifters are directory-discovered: one self-registering file per extractor, not a hardcoded Python dict.
Each lifter file must declare a module-level LIFTER dict with id matching the file stem and an extract callable (text, *, meta=None) -> value.
The live set currently contains three lifters: frontmatter, links, and blocks.
Malformed lifter entries must fail loudly at discovery; files without a LIFTER dict or starting with _ are skipped.
The file acts as a 'drift home' that must be updated when a lifter is added; tests/lifters_acceptance.py is said to enforce this.
A create_lifter authoring path is mentioned but explicitly flagged as a deferred wiring seam in runtime/authoring.py and Suite.create_lifter.
Discovery and reads are pure: for_projection(pid), as_records(), and extract are parse/read operations with no resolve/dispatch.
standouts: The registry-is-truth test is made concrete: adding a lifter must mean adding a file, not editing a dict.
Lifters are positioned as the code half of a projection split, complementing model-produced projections in the corpus capture.
Executable-code lifter authoring is gated like node-types, signaling it is treated as a privileged extension point.
conventions: Follows the constitution format with frontmatter type, register: prescriptive, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, status: living.
Mirrors existing registry patterns (roles, skills, projections, node-types) rather than inventing a new one.
Uses a drift-home list that must be kept in sync with the discovered file set.
Emphasizes pure reads and no dispatch/resolution inside extractors.
concerns: The drift-home requirement duplicates the directory state and relies on a test outside this file to prevent divergence.
The create_lifter authoring seam is referenced but not implemented here, leaving the actual extension path unspecified.
The optional produces and desc fields are mentioned but their semantics and schema constraints are not detailed.
notes: This is the constitution for the lifters registry: file-discovered deterministic extractors that feed code-produced corpus projections.
questions: Is the lifters/ directory located at company/lifters/ or elsewhere, and how does runtime/lifters.py discover it?
How exactly does for_projection(pid) map a projection id to a lifter id?
What does the acceptance test assert specifically about reflection in this file?
What are the allowed values and semantics of the produces field?
What is the intended behavior when two lifter files declare the same id?
How is the _build_lifter gate or render_lifter_source in runtime/authoring.py expected to work?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it documents the lifters registry, lists current lifters, and prescribes extension and discovery rules.
apparent_themes: deterministic extraction as a read-only floor
declarative authoring with gated executable extension
mirroring a single registry mechanism across modules
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a minimal, auditable, file-discovered extractor registry so structural corpus projections can be produced by code without model calls, while keeping the extension path as simple as dropping a file and updating the drift home.
novelty: The lifter concept as the deterministic code-half of a projection split, combined with a directory-discovered registry that must be reflected in a human-readable constitution and acceptance-tested.
contribution: A prescriptive contract for adding deterministic extractors via individual files, an explicit inventory of current structural lifters, and rules that keep code-produced projections discoverable and extension gated.
summary_for_embedding: Module constitution for the lifters registry, defining file-discovered, self-registering deterministic extractors (frontmatter, links, blocks) that produce code-generated corpus projections.
intention_for_embedding: To document and enforce the registry-is-truth pattern for structural extractors that feed the code-produced projection pipeline.
